title: Tighten rate limits in production config
description: Reduces the global API rate limit from 1000 to 200 req/min and adds per-endpoint overrides for the upload and search endpoints.
diff:
diff --git a/config.yaml b/config.yaml
index aaaaaaa..bbbbbbb 100644
--- a/config.yaml
+++ b/config.yaml
@@ -5,10 +5,14 @@ server:
   port: 8080
 
 rate_limiting:
-  global_rpm: 1000
+  global_rpm: 200
   burst_multiplier: 1.5
+  per_endpoint:
+    /api/v2/upload:
+      rpm: 20
+    /api/v2/search:
+      rpm: 50
   storage: redis
   redis_url: ${REDIS_URL}
 
@@ -18,4 +22,5 @@ logging:
   level: info
   format: json
+  include_request_id: true
human_notes: Reduces global rate limit 5x and adds per-endpoint caps. This is a production config change. Briefing should flag that existing clients hitting >200 req/min will start getting 429s. Key questions: were clients notified? Is the burst_multiplier still correct with the lower base? Is the Redis key-space for the new per-endpoint limits compatible with the existing rate-limiter implementation?
